name: HGH Fragment 176-191
subtitle: Growth Hormone Fragment | Fat Loss Peptide

modifications: Native hGH sequence from position 176 to 191
Disulfide bond between Cys183 and Cys189

overview: HGH Fragment 176-191 is the fat-burning segment of human growth hormone, corresponding to amino acids 176 through 191 of the full GH molecule. It stimulates lipolysis and inhibits lipogenesis without the growth-promoting or insulin-disrupting effects associated with full-length HGH. AOD-9604 is a modified version of this fragment with an added N-terminal tyrosine residue.
mechanism: Mimics the lipolytic region of human growth hormone by stimulating fat breakdown through beta-3 adrenergic receptor pathways. Unlike full HGH, it does not bind the growth hormone receptor, so it does not increase IGF-1 levels or interfere with glucose metabolism. The fragment selectively targets adipose tissue to promote fat oxidation.
keyBenefits: Stimulates lipolysis without growth hormone side effects
Does not affect blood glucose or insulin sensitivity
No impact on bone or organ growth

items.name: Lipolysis Enhancement
items.description: Increases fat oxidation and breakdown of stored triglycerides in adipose tissue without affecting glucose homeostasis.

items.name: Lipogenesis Inhibition
items.description: Reduces new fat synthesis and storage, complementing the lipolytic action for overall fat reduction.

items.name: Body Composition
items.description: Promotes selective fat loss while preserving lean mass, particularly effective when combined with a caloric deficit and exercise.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection is the standard route. Best administered twice daily on an empty stomach -- once in the morning and once before bed. Avoid eating for at least 30 minutes after injection for optimal fat-burning effect.

steps: Allow vial to reach room temperature (15-20 minutes)
Clean vial stopper with alcohol swab
Inject slowly down vial side (not directly onto powder)
Gently swirl until dissolved (never shake vigorously)
Store reconstituted solution at 2-8 degrees C

keyBenefits: Abdomen (near navel) or thigh injection sites
Wait 30 minutes before eating after injection

interactions.notes: Redundant lipolytic mechanism. Full HGH already contains the 176-191 sequence, so combining provides diminishing returns for fat loss while adding HGH-related side effects.
Same mechanism of action. AOD-9604 is a modified version of this fragment; using both is redundant and unnecessary.
Both target fat loss through complementary adrenergic pathways. The combination may enhance lipolysis beyond either compound alone.

timeline.effects: Minimal noticeable changes; fragment accumulating and establishing lipolytic signaling
Subtle improvements in fat loss, especially around the midsection with proper diet
More noticeable fat reduction and improved body composition with consistent use
Continued fat loss and body recomposition; most users run 8-12 week cycles

common: Headache, particularly in the first few days
Generally well-tolerated with a favorable safety profile
stopSigns: Severe injection site reactions or signs of infection
Hypoglycemia symptoms if combined with insulin (dizziness, sweating, confusion)
contraindications: Active cancer or history of malignancy
WADA prohibited - athletes subject to testing must avoid
monitoring: Blood glucose if combining with insulin or diabetes medications

good: White lyophilized powder with uniform appearance
Crystal clear solution without particles after reconstitution
Certificate of Analysis showing >98% purity by HPLC
warning: Not FDA approved - research chemical only
bad: Discolored or clumped powder (yellow indicates degradation)

references.title: Increase of fat oxidation and weight loss in obese mice caused by chronic treatment with human growth hormone or a modified C-terminal fragment
references.authors: Heffernan MA, Thorburn AW, Fam B, Summers R, Conway-Campbell B, Waters MJ, Ng FM
references.keyFindings: Both hGH and the C-terminal fragment (176-191) reduced body weight gain, increased fat oxidation, and stimulated lipolysis in obese ob/ob mice without affecting glucose metabolism.

references.title: The effects of human GH and its lipolytic fragment (AOD9604) on lipid metabolism following chronic treatment in obese mice and beta(3)-AR knock-out mice
references.authors: Heffernan MA, Jiang WJ, Thorburn AW, Ng FM
references.keyFindings: The hGH fragment and its modified form AOD-9604 both increased beta-3 adrenergic receptor expression in obese mice. Effects were abolished in beta-3-AR knockout mice, confirming this receptor pathway is essential for the lipolytic action.

references.title: Metabolic studies of a synthetic lipolytic domain (AOD9604) of human growth hormone
references.authors: Ng FM, Jiang WJ, Gianello R, Pitt C, Heffernan M
references.keyFindings: Characterized the metabolic properties of the hGH 176-191 lipolytic domain, demonstrating antilipogenic activity independent of the growth hormone receptor.

references.title: Effects of oral administration of a synthetic fragment of human growth hormone on lipid metabolism
references.authors: Heffernan MA, Heffernan MJ, Jiang WJ, Thorburn AW, Ng FM
references.journal: American Journal of Physiology - Endocrinology and Metabolism
references.keyFindings: Oral treatment with the synthetic hGH fragment for 30 days significantly reduced body weight gain and altered lipogenic and lipolytic activity in adipose tissue of obese mice.

latestResearch.title: Detection and in vitro metabolism of AOD9604
latestResearch.summary: Developed detection methods for AOD-9604 and related hGH fragments, relevant for anti-doping analysis and understanding in vivo metabolism of the 176-191 sequence.

quickStats.storage: Lyophilized: room temp or freezer long-term. Reconstituted: 2-8 degrees C for 28 days
